components.use_when: Users need stable location and top-level movement.
A dense workflow needs persistent scope or object context.
A result set has several visible query dimensions.
Two to five mutually exclusive modes share one surface.
Repeated object-level actions need compact access.
Structured input has validation and recovery behavior.
Records need precise comparison, sorting, or repeated actions.
Values change over an ordered time interval.
Options share explicit criteria and tradeoffs.
Sequence, milestones, or event causality are primary.
Users must scan health, freshness, and exceptions.
No data or no matching result needs explanation and a next action.
A focused interrupt must be completed or dismissed before return.
Users need object detail without losing list context.
A real product view, mechanism, or evidence must support a claim.
One primary decision and at most one secondary path end a section.
A browser presentation needs stable frame geometry and navigation.
Several interactive screens need shared navigation and simulated state.
A repository or product must remain identifiable at thumbnail size.
A developer tool is best explained by current terminal commands, state, and output.
Three to five concise, verified capabilities support a primary proof without becoming a card wall.
A deterministic time range carries one dominant product or narrative idea.
Motion, Canvas, WebGL, or remote media needs a complete static alternative.
Two or three visual directions must be judged against the same brief and geometry.
A small set of meaningful visual or interaction parameters must be compared live.
A mobile, desktop, or browser prototype needs target-platform geometry without decorative dominance.
A browser or exported presentation needs presenter-only narrative context.
An editable HTML-first PPTX needs a reserved native chart, table, or diagram insertion point.
Measured voice segments drive visual scene timing.
Licensed voice, music, and effects need explicit timing, gain, fade, and provenance.
One measured value must anchor a claim with unit, scope, and source.
Labels and callouts must explain structure, causality, or a process directly on the visual.
